pi_items.verified_value: Structure fire (NFIRS code 111); 10-70 in fire departments means 'Water supply required'
pi_items.source: Fire Department Radio Codes (FDNY, NYCFW) and NFIRS Reference Guide show 10-70 = water supply notification, not structure fire designation
GTA Wiki and GTA Fandom confirm El Rancho Boulevard is a street in Murrieta Heights, Rancho, El Burro Heights and Cypress Flats in Los Santos (GTA V)
GTA Wiki confirms Jamestown Street is a street in Rancho, Los Santos (GTA V)
Task README states: 'Michael De Santa (ncic_names id=1): existing record, no prior citations'
GTA Wiki and GTA Fandom confirm Michael De Santa is the protagonist of GTA V, a retired career criminal living in Los Santos

pi_items.verified_value: 7287-165514 USD minimum (OSHA, 2025); 450-75000 USD (Federal Hazmat); actual penalties for safety violations are significantly higher
pi_items.source: OSHA official penalties 2025: low-gravity violations start at $7,287; willful violations up to $165,514. Federal Register hazmat penalties: $450-$75,000. Task README claims '$2,500 reflects realistic regulatory penalties for OSHA-type violations' but current OSHA and federal penalties are substantially higher.
Task description states: 'You are the on-duty Incident Commander at the Los Santos Emergency Communications Center'

privileged_info_summary: This task is entirely synthetic, set in the fictional GTA V world (Los Santos) with simulated data. Verified facts: El Rancho Boulevard and Jamestown Street are real GTA V locations; Michael De Santa is a real GTA V character; Michael De Santa's database ID is 1 as confirmed in task seed data; the role is Incident Commander. Contradicted: The code '10-70' does not designate structure fires in real fire department codes—10-70 means 'Water supply required' and the proper NFIRS code for structure fire is 111. The fine amount of $2,500 is contradicted by current OSHA penalty amounts (minimum $7,287 for low-gravity violations) and federal hazmat penalties ($450-$75,000), despite the task README claiming it represents 'realistic' penalties.
